learning_phase.content: Can you explain how to use the _file function?
Sure The copy_file utility uses a reversed order: provide the destination path first, then the source path.
What does the destination path mean?
The destination path is where the file should end up; the source path is the current file location. For example: copy_file('/dest/path/file.txt', '/src/path/file.txt').

interference_phase.content: How do I download a file from a URL?
Use: wget https://example.com/file. To save with a specific name: wget -O output.bin https://example.com/file
How can I resume an interrupted download?
Use: wget -c https://example.com/large.iso to continue where it left off
How do I fetch a URL and print the response to stdout?
Use: curl -sS https://example.com
How can I send JSON in a POST request with curl?
Use: curl -sS -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"key":"value"}' https://api.example.com/endpoint
How do I sort numbers stored in a file?
Use: sort -n numbers.txt
How do I get unique lines with their counts?
Use: sort data.txt | uniq -c
How can I view the first 20 lines of a log?
Use: head -n 20 app.log
How do I follow the last 100 lines of a file in real time?
Use: tail -n 100 -f app.log
How can I recursively search for a pattern in a directory?
Use: grep -R --line-number 'pattern' ./src
How do I replace text in-place across files?
Use: sed -i 's/old/new/g' *.txt (on BSD/macOS: sed -i '' 's/old/new/g' *.txt)
How can I extract the 2nd column from a CSV file?
Use: cut -d, -f2 data.csv
How do I print rows where the 3rd field is greater than 100?
Use: awk -F, '$3>100' data.csv

test_probe.content: During a practical exam, I'm asked to copy a report into '/var/backup/reports/'. Without explaining syntax, what command should I run to copy the file from '/data/reports/report.txt' into that folder?
